relevant_files.summary: Server-side service for appending AI intent ledger entries; after writing the ledger entry it best-effort appends edit_intent audit events.
MCP tool registration for fab_append_intent; exposes the existing server interface for task-completion intent recording.
JSONL audit event infrastructure for get_rules, edit_intent, and rule_selection events.
Fetches selected rule sections and records rule_selection audit events with token, required ids, selected ids, final ids, and selection reasons.
Runs health checks, audit compliance checks, and meta hash drift detection. It detects rule file drift but does not currently record drift into ledger/audit as a mutation fact.
Mutation service for agents.meta.json registry operations; currently writes meta and invalidates cache without ledger/audit event recording.
Current public LedgerEntry types are minimal: AI entries include intent, affected paths, optional commit; human entries include parent sha and diff stat.
Canonical .fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl storage, legacy read fallback, append, and migration behavior.

patterns.pattern: Ledger append followed by best-effort audit append
patterns.description: Ledger write is primary; compliance telemetry failures are intentionally non-blocking.
Rules access and selection are stored as audit-event records, not Ledger entries.

patterns.pattern: Doctor hash drift detection is read-only
patterns.description: Doctor compares actual file hashes with meta hashes and returns drift counts/check messages without appending records.

patterns.pattern: Registry mutation has centralized server entrypoint
patterns.description: fab_update_registry is the intended path for registry metadata mutation and is a natural audit/ledger hook point.

constraints: Analyze-with-file is read-only; no source code was modified.
Ledger schema is currently intentionally small and consumed by dashboard/history/timeline flows.
Audit log parser is a typed union; adding event kinds requires parser, tests, and doctor consumers to be updated.
runDoctorReport is read-oriented. Any write-side drift recording should be explicit to avoid surprising worktree changes.
Current repository root lacks .fabric/agents.meta.json, so rule context MCP calls can fail before normal analysis. Doctor/report flows need graceful degradation.

integration_points.description: Primary task-completion intent recording and compliance event hook.
Rule selection telemetry hook after AI-selected L1 rules are validated.
Existing rule file hash drift detector; can feed a drift audit report or explicit record command.
Central registry mutation interface; should record metadata mutation details or at least append an audit event.

key_findings: Existing server APIs already record both ledger entries and audit events; the refactor should classify and complete those hooks rather than replace them.
Rules access/selection telemetry belongs in audit log; completed edits and important discovered drift facts belong in Ledger.
Doctor can detect rule file hash drift, but currently does not preserve old/current hash details in a durable record.
Registry mutation via fab_update_registry is unaudited today and is a high-leverage place to record rule metadata changes.

_metadata.exploration_scope: Ledger, audit-log, rule selection, doctor hash drift, registry mutation
